00001 /*
00002  * fork_syscall.c

00008 
00009 
00010 #include <types.h>
00011 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00012 #include <kern/errno.h>
00013 #include <lib.h>
00014 #include <thread.h>
00015 #include <current.h>
00016 #include <syscall.h>
00017 #include <spl.h>
00018 #include <mips/trapframe.h>
00019 
00020 /* fork duplicates the currently running process. The two copies are identical, except that one (the "new" one, or "child"), has * a new, unique process id, and in the other (the "parent") the process id is unchanged.
00021 * The process id must be greater than 0.
00022 * The two processes do not share memory or open file tables; this state is copied into the new process, and subsequent *modification in one process does not affect the other.
00023 * However, the file handle objects the file tables point to are shared, so, for instance, calls to lseek in one process can *affect the other. 
00024 */
00025 
00026 
00027 pid_t sys_fork(struct trapframe *tf, int *retval)
00028 {
00029         
00030         pid_t result = 0;
00031 
00032         result = thread_fork2(tf,retval);
00033         return result;
00034 }
